# Source Card: Diagnosis and management of isolated neutropenia: A survey of pediatric hematologist oncologists

## Summary
According to PubMed, this peer-reviewed survey of 126 US pediatric hematologist-oncologist attending physicians ([DOI](https://doi.org/10.1002/pbc.29946)) documents practice patterns — not consensus guidance — for isolated, asymptomatic, incidentally discovered pediatric neutropenia. It establishes that no consensus guidelines exist, maps the common diagnostic workup and its age-dependence, and identifies the factors clinicians weigh for referral and follow-up. For the pediatric CDS module it supplies referral-trigger and workup context, but it does NOT define any numeric ANC cutoff for mild/moderate/severe neutropenia.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) No consensus guidelines exist for isolated pediatric neutropenia — locator: Abstract, Background — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:b0ad4acd7c37242283dbd6938b56da062eaf73548d87de7bc3c79554578e04dd]"
- (ev_002) Common workup frequencies (CBC 98%, smear 75%, antineutrophil Ab 29%, Ig 24%) — locator: Abstract, Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:396ff7f263e038c65b4b8a9609fb5e52a58cbb2eb43608333b252493d47dcaec]"
- (ev_003) Age-dependent test selection (antineutrophil Ab in toddlers; ANA in adolescents) — locator: Abstract, Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:7d69b1669d64f774f81f9217680ba06d763aa414df35c730bae84f96c99b8092]"
- (ev_004) Refer mild neutropenia after confirming a declining ANC, not on a single value (51%) — locator: Abstract, Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:a7bd612c49bd1e620af5b545736f4e939ac99699667245e0fd3826ec3f2bed2b]"
- (ev_005) Top follow-up drivers: recurrent/severe infection hx (98%), family blood-disorder hx (98%), worsening neutropenia (97%) — locator: Abstract, Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:455fab1194dd485049ec9405a975f64782e74e41af6a7a1b7f82a8fe68ca813c]"
- (ev_006) 'Benign ethnic neutropenia' renaming to Fy(a-/b-) status (70% diagnosed; 75% support rename) — locator: Abstract, Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:0756465bbc19ed733a239ff23b70edc5be41d2405c8e80a9d133621ae978e1e4]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: pediatric patients (mixed ages, both sexes) with isolated, asymptomatic, incidentally discovered neutropenia; age bands referenced qualitatively ("toddlers", "adolescents") but not numerically bounded.
- Assay/method: survey reports test-ordering behavior, not analyzer-dependent cutoffs. The one method-specific item is red-cell phenotyping to confirm Fy(a-/b-) (Duffy-null) status (ev_006).
- Lifecycle: survey published 2022-12; no guideline version, effective date, or supersession applies (it is descriptive practice-pattern data, and explicitly notes the absence of guidelines).

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- Yes — this source is primarily about referral and follow-up decision drivers. ev_004 (refer mild neutropenia only after a confirmed declining ANC, per 51%) and ev_005 (recurrent/severe infection history, family blood-disorder history, and progressively worsening neutropenia as the top follow-up factors) are directly relevant to CDS referral-trigger / scope-boundary logic. These are practice patterns, not directive rules.

## Limitations & conflicts
- GAP: numeric ANC cutoffs for "mild"/"moderate"/"severe" neutropenia are NOT locatable in this source (abstract references "mild neutropenia" without defining a threshold value; PMC full text unavailable). Do NOT assert any ANC cutoff (e.g., 1.5/1.0/0.5 x10^9/L) from this source — obtain it from a reference-interval or hematology guideline source.
- All numeric values in this card are survey response percentages (practice-pattern statistics), not laboratory decision thresholds; they must not be converted into CDS lab cutoffs.
- Survey of self-reported US ASPHO-member practice (n=126); descriptive and subject to response/selection bias; does not constitute consensus guidance and may not generalize internationally.
- No direct conflict recorded with other sources; the "benign ethnic neutropenia" terminology debate (ev_006) is an open nomenclature question preserved as such, not resolved here.
